title: Exploring the electrophysiology of Parkinson's disease - magnetoencephalography combined with deep brain recordings from the subthalamic nucleus.
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous magnetoencephalography and subthalamic nucleus local field potential recordings from externalized deep brain stimulation patients with Parkinson's disease, acquired under dopaminergic medication on and off conditions. Participants performed two motor tasks—static forearm extension and self-paced fist clenching—each preceded by resting periods, with some patients recorded during rest only. The dataset enables investigation of basal ganglia-cortex circuit electrophysiology and its modulation by movement and pharmacological state.
methods_description: Data were collected from externalized DBS patients undergoing simultaneous MEG and STN LFP recordings. Two movement conditions were employed: (1) 5 minutes of rest followed by static forearm extension (hold), and (2) 5 minutes of rest followed by self-paced fist clenching (move), with pauses interspersed in movement segments. Recordings were performed with and without dopaminergic medication (MedOn/MedOff). Some patients were recorded in resting-state only.

funding_references.funder_name: The project was funded by ERA-NET Neuron for 3 years.
